cot_prefix: Okay, let's find out what Oleksander's wife's attitude was toward his stay with a lion in a cage. The question gives four options: indifferent, objective, opposed, or supportive. First, I need to look through the text for any mentions of Oleksander's wife's reaction. The passage says, "Oleksander's wife, Olena, was also very trusting. 'When he first told me about the plan I was very scared. But I decided to support my husband.'" So, she was initially scared but decided to support him. The key word here is "support my husband," which directly indicates that her attitude was supportive. Even though she was scared, she still supported the plan. The other options don't fit as well. She wasn't indifferent because she had a reaction (being scared) and made a choice. She wasn't opposed because she supported him after the initial
